description_long: This is a rugged, hand-crafted axe featuring a robust, roughly hewn stone blade secured to a wooden handle with thick, coarse twine. The blade is double-sided, with a rectangular profile and slightly uneven edges, suggesting it is made from natural stone rather than forged metal. The handle is straight, cylindrical, and appears to be made from a single piece of dark, weathered wood, showing visible grain and some wear at the grip end. The twine binding is thick and irregular, tightly wrapping the blade to the handle, giving the tool a primitive, utilitarian appearance. There are no visible manufacturer markings or decorative elements, emphasizing its functional, handmade nature. The overall look suggests a tool that could be used for heavy-duty chopping or demolition tasks in an urban or survival context.
description: A primitive, double-sided stone axe with a weathered wooden handle and thick twine binding the blade. The tool has a rugged, hand-crafted appearance with no visible markings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the axe, highlighting the rectangular stone blade and the twine-wrapped joint with the wooden handle.
description_view_1: End-on view of the handle, displaying the circular cross-section and the rough, worn texture of the wood.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the first, with the stone blade and twine binding clearly visible.
description_view_3: Blade-on view, showing the thickness of the stone blade and the way it is lashed to the handle with twine.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle, visibly weathered with a rough texture and no ergonomic grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Double-sided rectangular stone blade with uneven edges, no beard or poll, and a primitive, functional shape.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional, with the blade lashed to the handle using thick twine.
